description:
Roll-up epic for the parallel-path CORRECTNESS findings from the 2026-06-22 multi-persona critical review of the complete library (commit 196bb8c). Source report: ~/vaults/rmbrs-vault/code_review/critical/mutagen_ex/2026-06-22/complete-library/20260622-071209-consolidated.md

SCOPE (revised 2026-06-22 after the epic-review of mutagen-33b itself; report at .../2026-06-22/mutagen-33b-epic/): this epic is now the parallel-path correctness workstream ONLY. The coverage-driven test-selection optimization (former F2 / children .4 + .5) was SPLIT OUT into epic mutagen-xez because it is a performance optimization with the opposite risk profile (it ADDS a parity-proving correctness surface) and was needlessly gating the high-confidence correctness fixes. CF2b (mutagen-33b.3) stays here, deferred, and still depends cross-epic on mutagen-xez's F2b.

After calibration the review found 0 Critical / 2 High in the DEFAULT (--max-concurrency 1) config. The correctness Highs live on the opt-in parallel path: a killed worker can leave a module loaded as its mutant with no rollback (CF1), and concurrent workers share global ExUnit/:cover/code-server state and corrupt each other's kill/survive classification (CF2).

## Done = (gate + warn; real isolation deferred)
- A killed parallel worker never leaves a mutated module loaded (restore sweep on outer-task :exit, sweep ordered AFTER sibling teardown), proven by a non-vacuous worker-crash test.
- `--max-concurrency > 1` is honestly gated (warned-experimental) until OS-process-sharded parallelism (CF2b) lands.

## Children
- CF1 (mutagen-33b.1): restore-on-exit + non-vacuous crash test (P1).
- CF2a (mutagen-33b.2): gate --max-concurrency>1 as warned-experimental (P1).
- CF2b (mutagen-33b.3): safe parallelism via OS-process sharding (P2, DEFERRED). Build-gate MUST be given teeth: a named runtime threshold + an owner + a measurement trigger, else "parallel is experimental" becomes the permanent state. Depends cross-epic on mutagen-xez F2b (mutagen-33b.5).
- Capstone (mutagen-33b.6): Tier-1 correctness verified (P1).

## Deferred / out of scope (recorded for traceability)
- F2 coverage-driven selection -> epic mutagen-xez (re-tiered P2, behind a measured-need gate).
- CF4 (per-site VM snapshot O(host_size)x2S + anon-process blindness) was a consensus Medium NOT pulled into Tier 1: documented caveat, no parallel-path coupling, and its `snapshot once per loop` fix is independent. Belongs in the Tier-2 sweep.

comments.text: WORKTREE RENAME (2026-06-23): branch epic/33b -> mutagen-33b/tier1-review-hardening; worktree dir ../mutagen_ex-33b -> ../mutagen_ex-tier1-review-hardening. Stages mutagen-33b.1 and mutagen-33b.2 merge back into this renamed branch.

ORCHESTRATOR: mix spec.check is pre-existing red on the epic base (b31354f) for mutagen.ast + mutagen.cli (unrelated subjects) — tracked in mutagen-ua9. Per user decision, this epic's tickets are DELTA-GATED: compile --warnings-as-errors + mix test + own-subject spec verification + no NEW spec.check findings beyond those 2. CF1 (.1) and CF2a (.2) implementers being re-issued under the corrected gate (gate correction, not a quality bounce).

ORCHESTRATED
Children closed: 3 (mutagen-33b.1 CF1, mutagen-33b.2 CF2a, mutagen-33b.6 capstone)
BOUNCEs encountered: 0 (no quality bounces; the early WORKER_ERRORs were a poisoned project gate — mix spec.check pre-existing red — resolved by delta-gating, not a bounce; one noclobber re-spawn was an orchestrator wiring fix; one clean rebase of .1 over .2 for the shared test file)
User escalations: 0 (planning decisions only: epic base, worker mode, gate policy)
Deferred (not driven, per epic scope): mutagen-33b.3 CF2b (P2, blocked cross-epic by mutagen-33b.5, unactionable build-gate)
Pre-existing baseline tracked out-of-scope: mutagen-ua9 (mix spec.check red on mutagen.ast stale grep + mutagen.cli archive telemetry; plus stale scenario-5 baseline-red golden)

Epic branch mutagen-33b/tier1-review-hardening @ 249d377 contains both fixes, integrated, smoke + capstone green. Worker mode: Codex (personal) implementers; Claude verify/audit/rebase. Epic LEFT in_progress (phase:implementing) for the ship pipeline — epic-branch -> main PR is a deliberate human checkpoint.

GATE RE-EVALUATION (2026-06-24, after rebase onto current main 84f90a5):
- Epic rebased clean onto main; now 4 commits on top (CF2a, CF1, state-regen, spec-delta mutagen-33b.7). main is a clean ancestor. mix test 645/0, compile --warnings-as-errors clean, format + deps gates green.
- The original delta-gate is RETIRED: main's fixes resolved the old mutagen.ast/mutagen.cli verification_command_failed errors; spec.validate now passes errors=0 warnings=0.
- Bucket A (~29 overlap/duplicate_covers) ELIMINATED: main 22a48cf disabled that check for the facet-decomposed corpus (.spec/config.yml).
- Bucket B (branch-guard: code-without-spec) CLEARED by mutagen-33b.7 spec delta (r17 CF1 restore-sweep ordering, r18 CF2a experimental gate, decision parallel_experimental_gate).
- mix spec.check residual = 1 finding: [WARNING] branch_guard_unmapped_change README.md (README not mapped to any subject — repo-wide gap; config.yml line 51 sets this severity=warning). errors=0.

Net: spec.check has zero ERRORs; the only finding is a project-designated advisory warning about README subject-mapping, independent of this epic's correctness work.

title: [EPIC] Parallel-path correctness hardening (gate + warn; real isolation deferred)
